summary: RETEST of iteration_23 CRITICAL fix (PdfReader.jsx infinite re-render loop) — VERIFIED FIXED. Uploaded /app/backend/tests/fixtures/tiny.pdf on /read (unlock=mind-mapper67); within 3s 'Rendering PDF…' spinner disappears and [data-testid='reader-page-1'] renders the PDF content ('Hello Mind-Mapper…'). Console captured ZERO 'Maximum update depth exceeded' errors and ZERO pageerrors. Multi-tab flow works: opened a 2nd PDF via the reader-view hidden file input → both tabs render in [data-testid='reader-tabbar'] (reader-tab-<id1>, reader-tab-<id2>, reader-tab-add). Clicking back to the first tab shows [data-testid='reader-page-1'] instantly with spinner=0 even at 50ms post-click — confirms loadedOnceRef dedup + pdfDoc cache work (no re-load). Highlights panel regression clean (reader-highlights-panel + 'This session' heading intact). Regression routes /app, /share/fakeslug-xyz, /intake, /tools all mount with 0 pageerrors. Backend smoke: /api/health=200 {status:ok}, /api/billing/plans=200 returns both plans [monthly, annual], /api/share/anything/og.png=200 valid PNG 1200x630 43,819 bytes.
critical_code_review_comments: PdfReader.jsx L428-435: the second hidden <input type='file'> (reader-view) still has NO data-testid attribute — only the dropzone input (L311-319) has data-testid='reader-file-input'. This input gets the fileInputRef assignment last, so after a session loads the visible dropzone unmounts and only this non-testid'd input remains in the DOM. Adding data-testid='reader-file-input' to BOTH inputs would make Playwright uploads uniform (currently required fallback to `input[type="file"]`.last in tests). Non-blocking but hinders automation robustness — called out in iter_23 review as well, still not addressed.

PdfReader.jsx L116 eslint-disable react-hooks/exhaustive-deps is intentional and documented in the comment — acceptable given loadedOnceRef guards re-entry and sessions.find() inside the effect reads fresh state at mount. Fix is sound.
loadedOnceRef delete on error (L111) correctly allows tab-switch retry; good defensive pattern.
test_credentials: Access gate: REACT_APP_UNLOCK_KEY=mind-mapper67 (also accepts ?unlock=mind-mapper67 in URL — used in tests to bypass gate modal).
seed_data_creation: Reused /app/backend/tests/fixtures/tiny.pdf (1472 bytes) created in iteration_23. Copied to /tmp/tiny2.pdf for the second-tab test so the two sessions have distinct filenames in the tab bar.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Infinite re-render loop in PdfReader.jsx is conclusively fixed. The keyed-on-activeSessionId useEffect with loadedOnceRef Set (lines 92-117) correctly loads each PDF exactly once and avoids the patchSession feedback loop. Tab switching is instant from cache. The only outstanding (non-blocking) issue is the missing data-testid on the reader-view hidden file input at L428 — if you need to upload a 2nd PDF via Playwright, use `page.locator('input[type="file"]').last.set_input_files(...)` in reader view.
rca of the issue: Original bug (iter_23): useEffect depended on the memoised activeSession object, and each patchSession produced a new sessions array → new activeSession identity → effect re-fired → patchSession again → 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. Fix applied (iter_24): dependency reduced to [activeSessionId] only (stable primitive) + loadedOnceRef Set guards the effect body so each session id triggers getDocument exactly once, even across unrelated sessions[] identity changes. Verified by (a) PDF rendering to reader-page-1 within 3s, (b) zero 'Maximum update depth exceeded' entries in console, (c) instant tab-switch from cache with no spinner flash.
